---
title: "Participant Prep Guide"
subtitle: "What to install before the AI Bootcamp"
---

## Before Session 1 (April 22)

Please install the following before the first session. If you get stuck, don't worry — we'll help during the session.

### Required

**Claude Code** (terminal-based AI assistant)

1. Open your terminal (Terminal.app on Mac, or Ghostty if installed)
2. Run: `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`
3. Run: `claude` and follow the login prompts
4. You'll need an [Anthropic account](https://console.anthropic.com/) (free to create; usage is pay-as-you-go after free tier)

::: {.callout-note}
If you don't have `npm`, install [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) first (LTS version). On Mac: `brew install node` if you have Homebrew.
:::

### Recommended

**Ghostty** — a fast, clean terminal app

- Download from [ghostty.org](https://ghostty.org)
- Why? Nicer than the default Terminal.app. Not required, but makes the demo easier to follow.

**Typora** — a markdown editor/viewer

- Download from [typora.io](https://typora.io)
- Why? Claude Code outputs markdown files. Typora renders them beautifully so you can review, edit, and share.

### Already have these? You're set.

If you already use VS Code, Claude Code also works as a [VS Code extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=anthropics.claude-code). You can use whichever interface you prefer.

## What to bring

- Laptop (Mac or Windows) with power cable
- A real task you'd like to try — a syllabus to reformat, data to explore, an email to draft, an assignment to create

## Cost

- **Claude Code**: Free tier available; pay-as-you-go after that (~$3–10/month for moderate use)
- **Ghostty**: Free
- **Typora**: $14.99 one-time (free trial available)
- **UVM Microsoft Copilot**: Free for all UVM — [go.uvm.edu/copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot)

PDF funds may cover AI subscriptions. Ask your department chair.
